svg.title: Attestation bridge anatomy — what it is, who does each part, what is unoccupied
svg.desc: Four horizontal bands showing the anatomy of the attestation bridge: institutional compliance systems at top, the translation and publication layer in the middle split into institution-run and shared infrastructure, the on-chain EAS layer, and existing analogues shown for comparison at the bottom.
svg.text: EXISTING — each institution already has these. no changes needed.
THE BRIDGE — this is what does not exist yet as an open standard
each institution runs its own certified instance
shared infrastructure — who governs this is the open question

g.text: EAS — on-chain attestation · institution wallet signs · 15+ EVM networks · $0.07 / attestation on L2
✓ exists · ✓ open source · ✓ no vendor dependency

svg.text: CLOSEST ANALOGUES TODAY — doing adjacent things, not this
open-source bridge software + EEA certification + GLEIF accreditation of issuers + neutral consortium governance
model: SWIFT for messaging format — not SWIFT as operator. each bank runs certified instance. EEA owns the spec.
no one is building this today. it is the precondition for Shibui working at institutional scale.
